📍 Oxford, UK
👋 Hi!

Oxford has some, odd, fun history!

Everyone knows Oxford for its University and ancient colleges — but the city that surrounds these also has a fantastic, varied history. It was even once England's capital! (take that London!)

Big Friendly Tours is all about bringing this history to life from a locals point of view. From what the Saxons got up to, to what our guides got up to growing up here — from Kings to Orcs, from Harry Potter to Radiohead, we cover more than you would think!

🗺️ Join the adventure
🎒 What's the tour like?

Everything you need to know

🕰️

About 90-120 minutes

Perfect to get you started and get your bearings in the city. Tour length varies depending on group size, local events and just how carried away our guides get with stories!

👟

100% local

All of our guides are either from here or long term residents. We know all the best spots and have stories of our lives here.

📍

Meet on Ship Street

We meet right in the centre of the city on Ship Street, OX1 3DE — opposite New Ground Coffee. We'll be the ones holding the orange umbrellas!

📅

Thursday, Friday and Saturday

Our current tours start at: 11am Thursday, Friday & Saturday · 2pm Thursday and Friday. Please note this can change — check closer to your tour date or on our Insta.

💷

"Free" walking tour

We have no set price — instead please tip your guide whatever you feel the tour was worth. We have card machines but Oxford's internet is notoriously bad, so cash is better!

🔒

Best to book, walk-ups welcome

We cap group sizes for everyone's benefit so it's best to book ahead. However, so long as we have space, walk-ups are more than welcome.

🙋 Your guides and the tour

100% Local!

All our guides are Oxford locals — some grew up here, some moved here and have lived and worked here for some time. This means we can really give an in-depth view of our home, the good and the bad! And yes, we definitely know the best place for a pint or coffee (or both, if you reeeally like excitement).


Our local University and city walk takes you around the old heart of the city, around the Colleges, through the town's centre and to all the main sights. The route and tour will vary but we'll keep it interesting every time.

🎟️ Walk with us

❓ FAQ

Got questions? We've got answers!

What is a "free" tour?+
If you travel a lot you may already have done one before — if not, that's OK! Instead of charging a flat fee we ask for tips. You can donate whatever you like. Our guides can take card payments, but Oxford's notoriously bad internet means cash is often better and faster!
Should I book? Are walk-ups ok?+
We cap group sizes to avoid overcrowding and so everyone can hear the tour, so booking ahead is advised. However, should we have space, walk-ups are more than welcome. Maximum booking size is 6 people.
Is it suitable for kids?+
Mostly yes — given Oxford's history and the cast of characters that have passed through, there will be some adult themes and stories less suitable for younger children.
What should I bring / wear?+
The weather can do pretty much anything in Oxford, though it's usually mild most of the year. Good comfy shoes are definitely recommended. In summer bring water and suncream — in cooler months a waterproof jacket and umbrella never hurt!
What if I'm late or wish to cancel?+
If you wish to cancel please contact us. Tours start at 11am and 2pm — if booked guests are late we wait up to 5 minutes, then we must start out of fairness to everyone else. Luckily introductions take a few minutes so look out for the ORANGE UMBRELLA!
Is the route accessible?+
The tour is a 90–120 minute walk around a mostly flat medieval city. There are some cobbles, road crossings and bumpy streets. Should you have specific access questions please get in touch.
Where do you go?+
Our tour takes you for 90–120 minutes around the publicly accessible parts of Oxford. No entry to ticketed attractions is included — so no internal access to the Bodleian Library, Christ Church, New College or other paid locations. No "free" tour offers internal college or library access — any that claim this are a scam!
Can I get a private tour?+
We're happy to arrange a private tour — please contact us directly for details.
What language is the tour in?+
Our tours are conducted in English. If you'd like a tour in another language please get in touch and we'll do our best to accommodate you.
